# Simple SchNet-based model for HOMO-LUMO gap prediction
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch_geometric.nn import SchNet
from torch_geometric.data import Batch

class HOMOLUMOPredictor(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_channels=128, num_filters=128, num_interactions=6, num_gaussians=50):
        super(HOMOLUMOPredictor, self).__init__()
        
        self.schnet = SchNet(
            hidden_channels=hidden_channels,
            num_filters=num_filters,
            num_interactions=num_interactions,
            num_gaussians=num_gaussians,
            cutoff=10.0
        )
        
        # Get the output size of SchNet (which is hidden_channels)
        self.output_layer = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(hidden_channels, 64),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(64, 1)
        )
    
    def forward(self, z, pos, batch=None):
        # z: atomic numbers (shape: [num_nodes])
        # pos: 3D positions (shape: [num_nodes, 3])
        # batch: batch indices (shape: [num_nodes]) - optional
        
        # The SchNet.forward method expects z (atomic numbers) and pos (positions)
        # It returns graph-level embeddings after internal pooling
        # NOTE: We don't pass batch here as SchNet handles batching internally
        graph_embeddings = self.schnet(z, pos, batch=batch)
        
        # Apply output layer to get final prediction
        output = self.output_layer(graph_embeddings)
        
        return output.squeeze(-1)  # Remove last dimension to get scalar prediction per graph
